title: Online state-space tyre-state features
summary: A new F1 tyre-degradation paper models latent tyre pace and degradation as an online state-space process with pit-stop resets, which is closer to the real decision variable than static tyre-life thresholds.
rationale: A new F1 tyre-degradation paper models latent tyre pace and degradation as an online state-space process with pit-stop resets, which is closer to the real decision variable than static tyre-life thresholds. That should add signal beyond the current hand-built finish-window heuristics.
implementation_hint: For each Driver x Race_Year sequence, fit a simple causal Kalman/state-space filter on lap-time residuals using only current/past laps; emit latent degradation level, latent degradation slope, filtered residual variance, and post-stop reset strength as features. Build all train features prefix-only inside each fold.
expected_effect: Better discrimination near ambiguous stop windows, especially when TyreLife is similar but actual pace loss differs.
risk: Easy to leak if the filter is smoothed with future laps; keep it strictly filtered, not bidirectional, and validate by held-out Race_Year/race groups.
